name: IOS
summary:
IOS
Code to exploit/patch IOS on the Nintendo Wii for certain features (ex. disabling AHBPROT, restoring trucha bug, etc.)

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with bx_lr/android_rootkit_simple (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with bx_lr/android_local_priv (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with adeleda/ios_forensic_ramdisks (score=10); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['shell_android', 'shell_ios', 'web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 4: same attack_surface=['shell_android', 'shell_ios', 'web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 5: same attack_surface=['shell_android', 'shell_ios', 'web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use:
Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [shell_android, shell_ios, web] in the Mobile Security category. Fits when the engagement needs: IOS
Code to exploit/patch IOS on the Nintendo Wii for certain features (ex. disabling AHBPROT, restoring trucha bug, etc.).
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: IOS Code to exploit/patch IOS on the Nintendo Wii for certain features (ex. disabling AHBPROT, restoring trucha bug, etc.).
documentation.how_to_use: Clone or install `IOS` from its upstream repository, then run `IOS --help` (or the README entrypoint) to confirm flags. IOS Code to exploit/patch IOS on the Nintendo Wii for certain features (ex. disabling AHBPROT, restoring trucha bug, etc.).

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases:
When you need Mobile Security, use IOS because IOS
Code to exploit/patch IOS on the Nintendo Wii for certain features (ex. disabling AHBPROT, restoring trucha bug, etc.).
When chaining Mobile Security tools, pair IOS with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `IOS --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
